steps.title: Identify the claim
steps.content: Toomer uses figurative language to connect Washington, DC, with the rural South. The right quotation must use figurative language to bridge those two places.

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content:
Choice A describes a southern wind carrying soil of my homeland — the rural South — that falls like a fertile shower on the streets of Washington, DC. The simile literally links the two places through the figure of a fertile rain.

B and C describe DC alone. D describes train sounds in a valley but doesn't connect DC and the South.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
